dossier.overview:
A `.charbin` file is a portable, static character package for Pokémon Resort. It bundles identity metadata, embedded PNG sprite sheets, logical actions (idle, walk, pause, …), and optional dialogue — everything the C++ overworld loader needs without calling SPMK or the network at runtime.

Charbins are not save files. Map placement, spawn coordinates, party state, and other runtime fields must never appear inside a package. SPMK authors packages; the game reads them from `pokemon-resort/assets/characters/{playable|npc|pokemon|objects}/{id}.charbin`.

Canonical schema source: `spmk/docs/CHARBIN_SCHEMA.md` (SPMK copies it into the library folder for C++). This article is the Resort Docs walkthrough with SPMK UI captures.

sections.title: Library layout
sections.summary: Where packages live and what each folder means.

blocks.body: SPMK Characters view (`./spmk run` → http://127.0.0.1:8788). The library scans `pokemon-resort/assets/characters/` by default, grouped into playable, NPC, Pokémon (by generation), and objects. Toggle **Show sprite previews** to load thumbnails lazily per generation.
blocks.caption: SPMK character library — one .charbin per card

blocks.html:
Foldermetadata.characterTypeTypical use
playable/playerCustom player avatars
npc/npcShop clerks, story NPCs, dialogue
pokemon/pokemonFollowers, box Pokémon, overworld species
objects/objectSigns, PCs, Poké Balls, animated props

blocks.body:
Filename rule: `{id}.charbin` where `id` matches the package JSON `id` slug (`^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$`). SPMK moves files when `characterType` changes. Flat `*.charbin` at the library root are migrated into subfolders on scan.

Each file is self-contained: PNG bytes are embedded in the binary asset table. The game does not need SPMK `workspace/` assets to render a character loaded from charbin.

sections.title: Schema reference
sections.summary: Binary container, JSON contract, and sprite profiles.

blocks.body: Little-endian container. Magic `SPMKCHAR` (8 bytes), `formatVersion` u32 = 1, `jsonLength` u32, UTF-8 JSON package, `assetCount` u32, then repeated asset entries. Each asset: length-prefixed `assetId`, length-prefixed `mime` (typically `image/png`), u32 `blobLength`, raw PNG bytes. JSON references assets only by `assetId` strings. Trailing bytes after the last asset are an error.

blocks.html:
OffsetSizeField
08magic = SPMKCHAR
84formatVersion (uint32) = 1
124jsonLength (uint32)
16NUTF-8 JSON package (N = jsonLength)
16+N4assetCount (uint32)
…variableAsset table (see below)

Each asset entry: assetId (u32 len + UTF-8) · mime (u32 len + UTF-8) · blobLength (u32) · PNG bytes.

blocks.title: Read order
blocks.caption: SPMK write path mirrors this order; C++ loader validates magic, version, and no trailing bytes
blocks.source:
flowchart LR
    A[SPMKCHAR magic] --> B[formatVersion 1]
    B --> C[Parse JSON package]
    C --> D[Load embedded PNGs by assetId]
    D --> E[Resolve actions to sheets + profile anims]
    E --> F[Runtime CharacterSpriteDefinition]

blocks.caption: write_charbin — serialize JSON + sorted embedded assets
blocks.body:
def write_charbin(package: Dict[str, Any], assets: Dict[str, bytes]) -> bytes:
    payload = json.dumps(package, ensure_ascii=False, separators=(",", ":")).encode("utf-8")
    parts = [MAGIC, struct.pack("<II", FORMAT_VERSION, len(payload)), payload]
    table_ids = sorted(assets.keys())
    parts.append(struct.pack("<I", len(table_ids)))
    for asset_id in table_ids:
        blob = assets[asset_id]
        mime = "image/png"
        parts.append(_pack_str(asset_id))
        parts.append(_pack_str(mime))
        parts.append(struct.pack("<I", len(blob)))
        parts.append(blob)
    return b"".join(parts)

blocks.caption: loadCharacterDefinition — game-side binary reader

blocks.body: Top-level contract (`schemaVersion` 1). Unknown keys on import are preserved — deep merge does not strip forward-compatible fields. Use `custom` bags at the root and under `metadata` / `dialogue` for extensions today; bump `schemaVersion` when you introduce breaking top-level shape changes.

blocks.html:
FieldTypeRequiredNotes
schemaVersionnumberyesMust be 1 today
packageTypestringyesAlways "character"
idstringyesSlug ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ → filename
displayNamestringyesUI / game label
internalNamestringyesEngine name; often same as id
baseProfilestringyescharacter, pokemon_small, pokemon_large, object
metadataobjectyesSee metadata table below
spriteSheetsarrayyesMay be [] for empty draft
actionsarrayyesLinks logic → sheet + animation name
dialogueobjectyeslines, packs, custom
relationshipsarraynoReserved; default []
unlockanynoReserved; default null
customobjectnoForward-compatible extension bag

Forbidden root keys (runtime / map state — validator errors if present):

mapId, map, position, worldPosition, spawn, spawnPoint, currentDirection, currentMap, partyState, runtimeState, eventState, x, y, z, tileX, tileY

metadata fieldTypeWhenPurpose
characterTypestringallplayer, npc, pokemon, object
pokemonId, speciesNamenumber / stringPokémonDex identity
pokemonSizestringPokémonsmall or large (64×64 cells on 256×256 sheet)
pokeapiobjectPokémonCached PokéAPI snapshot (no image URLs)
objectAnimatedbooleanObjectHint: play vs static when both exist
personality, likes, tagsstring[]NPCStory chips (UI clears for player)
partnerPokemonobject|nullNPCMain partner species reference
customobjectallExtension point — e.g. overworldSpriteKeys, bodyMarkers

spriteSheets[]TypeRules
idstringUnique within package (e.g. walk, walk_42_shiny)
assetIdstringMust exist in embedded asset table (e.g. walk_png)
profilestringDefaults to baseProfile
profileOverridesobjectOverride columns, rows from profile
animationsobjectPer-sheet frame overrides (frames, frameTimeMs, cells)

actions[]TypeNotes
idstringUnique (e.g. idle, walk, pause, play)
typestringidle, movement, or legacy walk
sheetIdstringMust match a spriteSheets[].id
animationNamestringKey in sprite profile animations or sheet override
movementDrivenbooleantrue for walk-style actions

blocks.body: Sprite profiles define grid geometry, direction rows, default animation frame lists, and rendering hints. SPMK ships built-in profiles in `spmk_app/data/sprite_profiles.json`. The game mirrors them in `pokemon-resort/config/gameplay/sprites/sprite_profiles.json` — keep both aligned when changing frame layout.

blocks.html:
Profile keyGridUse
character4×4 × 32pxTrainers, NPCs, playable humans
pokemon_small4×4 × 32pxSmall Pokémon overworld
pokemon_large4×4 × 64px on 256×256Large Pokémon overworld
object4×4 × 32px (row 0 only)Map props — static / play animations

DirectionSheet rowWalk frames
south (down)0columns 0–3 on that row
west (left)1columns 0–3
east (right)2columns 0–3
north (up)3columns 0–3

Standard trainer sheet: 128×128 PNG (4×4 cells of 32×32). Pokémon profiles define walk (4 frames) and pause (frame 0 only) — there is no profile-level idle; the package idle action points at walk for Gen-style bob while standing.

blocks.body: Package detail in SPMK: selected walk sheet thumbnail, direction previews (idle + walk × four facings), and action list. Canvas previews use nearest-neighbor scaling — pixel-perfect like the game.
blocks.caption: Sheet + direction animation previews

blocks.html:
characterTypeRecommended actions
npc / playeridle → idle anim · walk → walk anim (movementDriven: true)
pokemonidle → walk anim (bob) · pause → frame 0 · walk → movement
objectplay only — row-major 4×4, non-looping; no movement actions

sections.title: Pokémon multi-sheet merge
sections.summary: One .charbin per species — forms, shiny, swim, and eating layers.

blocks.body: Batch import modal: one character type per batch, filename-driven form and animation layer parsing. Plain `SPECIES.png` can create or replace base walk; any form or layer suffix merges into the existing `{species}.charbin`.
blocks.caption: SPMK batch import — merges into one file per species

blocks.html:
Filename patternSheet id example
PSYDUCK.pngwalk
GARCHOMP_female.pngwalk_female
ARCEUS_1.pngwalk_1
ALCREMIE_42_shiny_swim.pngwalk_42_shiny_swim

Layer order in suffixes: form → shiny → swim → eating. UI animationVariant field can add layers without renaming files. Metadata extension keys under metadata.custom: overworldSpriteKeys, overworldFormIds, bodyMarkers (accessory / sleep anchor rects per facing).

blocks.body: Import modes: `create` replaces the whole package only for plain base import (`SPECIES.png`, no form, no layers). `add` merges base walk only. Imports with any form or animation layer always merge — they never wipe sibling sheets on the same species file.

sections.title: Authoring with SPMK
sections.summary: Create, edit, validate, and save packages.

blocks.body: Opening a library card loads the draft editor: metadata fields, PokéAPI autofill (species snapshot embedded in JSON — no runtime API calls), embedded sheet upload, live direction previews, and **Save to library**.
blocks.caption: Package detail editor

blocks.body:
Quick start:

1. `cd spmk && ./spmk run` → http://127.0.0.1:8788
2. Default library: `pokemon-resort/assets/characters/` (override with **Change folder…** or `SPMK_CHARACTERS_DIR`)
3. **＋ Pokémon** / **＋ NPC** / **Batch import…** / click an existing card
4. Upload walk sheet PNG (SPMK normalizes 64→32 or 512→256 for large species)
5. **Save to library** → writes `{id}.charbin` into the type subfolder
6. `POST /api/packages/validate` (or save flow) returns `{ ok, errors[], warnings[] }`

Debug export: `POST /api/packages/export/debug-loose` writes `{id}.character.json` plus extracted `assets/{assetId}.png` for inspection without parsing binary.

blocks.html:
APIPurpose
GET /api/packages/libraryList scanned packages
POST /api/packages/draft/open-pathOpen existing file into editor
POST /api/packages/draft/assetUpload PNG (assetId)
POST /api/packages/saveWrite draft to disk
POST /api/packages/validateRun validator
POST /api/packages/batch/import-spritesMultipart batch PNG import
GET /api/packages/pokemon/lookup?q=PokéAPI autofill
GET /api/packages/profilesSprite profile definitions

sections.title: How to extend the schema
sections.summary: Forward-compatible bags, new profiles, and version bumps.

blocks.body:
Prefer extension without breaking existing packages:

1. **`custom` objects** — Add keys under root `custom`, `metadata.custom`, or `dialogue.custom`. SPMK merge preserves unknown keys. Game code can read optional fields without a schemaVersion bump.

2. **New metadata fields** — Add optional keys to `metadata` in `character_package.py` validation docs first. Keep them optional so old charbins still validate.

3. **New sprite profile** — Add an entry to `spmk_app/data/sprite_profiles.json` and mirror it in `pokemon-resort/config/gameplay/sprites/sprite_profiles.json`. Teach the C++ loader to resolve the new `baseProfile` key.

4. **New action ids** — Add actions pointing at existing profile animation names, or add animation overrides on the sheet. Follower / overworld systems select actions by id at runtime (e.g. `pause`, `sleep_*`).

5. **`schemaVersion` bump** — Reserve for breaking top-level renames or required-field changes. Bump `formatVersion` only if the binary container changes (today both are 1). Update `_SUPPORTED_SCHEMA` in SPMK, C++ magic/version checks, and this doc in the same change.

6. **Sync canonical markdown** — Edit `spmk/docs/CHARBIN_SCHEMA.md` whenever package code changes; SPMK copies it to `assets/characters/CHARBIN_SCHEMA.md` in the game repo on save/scan.

blocks.title: Extension decision flow
blocks.caption: Default to custom bags; bump versions only when loaders must reject old files
_FORBIDDEN_ROOT_KEYS — runtime fields that must never ship in a package

sections.title: Validation & game wiring
sections.summary: What SPMK checks and how Resort resolves paths.

blocks.html:
CheckSeverity
Unsupported schemaVersionerror
Forbidden runtime root keyserror
Invalid id slugerror
Unknown baseProfile or sheet profileerror
Duplicate sheet / asset idserror
Missing embedded assetId byteserror
Animation frame out of grid rangeerror
Action references unknown sheetIderror
character profile missing idle/walkwarning
Pokémon missing pause or idle not targeting walkwarning
Action animationName not in profilewarning

blocks.body:
Game resolution examples:

• Follower species → `assets/characters/pokemon/{species}.charbin` (`FollowerConfig.cpp`)
• Poké Ball prop → `assets/characters/objects/{ball_id}.charbin` (fallback `poke_ball.charbin`)
• Owmap placed characters → `loadCharacterDefinition` in `JsonOverworldLoader.cpp`

SPMK workspace sprites (`workspace/assets/`) are a separate legacy pipeline — they do not ship inside charbins unless exported via **Save to library**.

blocks.caption: SPMK Characters UI captures (Playwright from ./spmk run)

blocks.body:
In-repo references:

• `spmk/docs/CHARBIN_SCHEMA.md` — canonical schema (update with every package code change)
• `spmk/README.md` — `./spmk run`, library paths, batch workflow
• `pokemon-resort/assets/characters/CHARBIN_SCHEMA.md` — synced copy for C++ contributors
